expectations.evidence: Line 6-7: 'Standard: KWCAG2.2 (Korean Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) -- 33 items' and 'Reference Document: skills/accessibility-review/references/kwcag22.md'

All 33 items listed across auto-detection categories: O (3 items: #7, #25, #29), Triangle (22 items: #1,3,4,5,8,10-15,17-19,21-24,26,28,30,31,33), X (6 items: #2,6,9,16,20,27). Structure overview at line 36 confirms '4 Principles: Perceivable (9 items), Operable (15 items), Understandable (7 items), Robust (2 items)' = 33 total.

Lines 139-146 define the classification: 'O = Pass', 'X = Fail', 'Triangle = Advisory', '- = N/A', '판정불가 = Runtime verification not possible'. Items organized by O/Triangle/X detection levels throughout.

suggestions: No timing.json was found for this run, which is inconsistent with the other 3 runs. The output is again a simulation/methodology document rather than a direct audit, similar to eval-23/with_skill.
eval_feedback.overall: All 3 assertions pass. Despite the English prompt, the skill correctly guided the output to reference KWCAG2.2, enumerate all 33 items, and use O/X/Triangle classification. The skill's value is clearly demonstrated here compared to the without_skill run.
